**Final Approach (Assuming Lean has the Right Definitions):**

2. Use the chain rule to differentiate `nth_partial i f (γ(t))` with respect to `t`. 

### Abstract Step-by-Step Plan

1. **Define `γ(t) = (1 - t)x + ty` as the interpolator.**
2. **Write `interpolated_deriv` as `∑_i c_i * ∂f/∂i (γ(t))` where `c_i = y_i - x_i`.**
3. **Differentiate to get `∑_i c_i * d/dt [∂f/∂i (γ(t))]`.**
4. **Compute `d/dt [∂f/∂i (γ(t))] = ∑_j c_j * ∂²f/∂i∂j (γ(t))` using the chain rule.**
5. **Substitute back to get `∑_i ∑_j c_i c_j * ∂²f/∂i∂j (γ(t))`, which is exactly `interpolated_deriv2`.**

have h_main : deriv (interpolated_deriv x y f) = interpolated_deriv2 x y f := by
    have h1 : deriv (interpolated_deriv x y f) = (λ t => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (deriv (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
      have h2 : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt (interpolated_deriv x y f) (∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (deriv (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) t := by
        intro t
        have h3 : HasDerivAt (fun t => interpolator x y t) (y - x) t := by
          have h4 : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y) (y - x) t := by
            -- Prove that the interpolator has the derivative (y - x)
            sorry
          convert h4 using 1
          <;> simp [interpolator]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> ext <;> simp [smul_eq_mul]
        -- Use the chain rule to find the derivative of (f ∘ interpolator x y)
        sorry
      have h3 : deriv (interpolated_deriv x y f) = (λ t => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (deriv (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
        -- Use the fact that the derivative is unique
        sorry
      exact h3
    have h2 : (λ t => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (deriv (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) = interpolated_deriv2 x y f := by
      funext t
      have h3 : (∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (deriv (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) = ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the derivative of the j-th partial derivative is the i-j-th mixed partial derivative
        sorry
      have h4 : ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) = interpolated_deriv2 x y f t := by
        -- Use the definition of interpolated_deriv2 to rewrite the sum
        sorry
      calc
        (∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (deriv (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) = ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by rw [h3]
        _ = interpolated_deriv2 x y f t := by rw [h4]
    rw [h1, h2]
  exact h_main

have h₁ : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt (interpolated_deriv x y f) (∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) t := by
    intro t
    have h₂ : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) (∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (derivativeAt (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) t)) t := by
      -- Differentiate the sum term by term
      have h₃ : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) (∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (derivativeAt (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) t)) t := by
        -- Use the fact that the sum of differentiable functions is differentiable and differentiate term by term
        have h₄ : ∀ i : Fin n, HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) ((y i - x i) * (derivativeAt (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) t)) t := by

            -- Use the fact that the function is differentiable to get the derivative
            apply DifferentiableAt.hasDerivAt
            apply DifferentiableAt.comp _ (differentiableAt_const _ |>.add (differentiableAt_id _ |>.smul (differentiableAt_const _)))

                apply Differentiable.add
                · apply Differentiable.smul
                  · apply differentiable_const

                    · apply differentiable_id

            exact h₆ |>.comp t h₈

          have h₁₀ : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) ((y i - x i) * (derivativeAt (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) t)) t := by
            apply HasDerivAt.const_mul (y i - x i) h₅
          exact h₁₀
        -- Sum the derivatives over all i
        have h₁₁ : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) (∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (derivativeAt (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) t)) t := by
          -- Use the fact that the derivative of a sum is the sum of the derivatives
